description: Improve task-related learning recall by backfilling applicability metadata (task types, file patterns, error patterns), auto-populating metadata on future learnings, adding tag-aware retrieval scoring, and indexing tags in FTS5.
dualRepoNote: This effort spans TWO repos. Track B (Rust) commits go to linked_projects/task-mgr. Track A tasks (FEAT-001 SQL backfill, FEAT-004 parser) commit to external-ref (the host repo). The agent must cd to the correct repo for each task.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Happy path test: learn with task_id auto-populates applies_to_files from task_files
Happy path test: learn with task_id auto-populates applies_to_task_types from task prefix
Edge case: explicit --files flag overrides auto-population
Edge case: explicit --task-types flag overrides auto-population
Edge case: task with no task_files skips file auto-populate, still populates task type
Edge case: empty JSON array [] treated same as NULL for auto-populate

Known-bad discriminator: learn() with task_id='FEAT-003' populates applies_to_files with FEAT-003's actual task_files from DB, not a hardcoded or empty value — a stub returning ['src/main.rs'] must fail
Tests compile (may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until implementation)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for the learn command auto-population feature (B1/FR-003). Tests define expected behavior: when learn is called with task_id but without explicit --files/--task-types, the fields should be auto-filled from task context via resolve_task_context().
edgeCases: Partial metadata: learning has existing applies_to_files but empty applies_to_task_types — only populate the empty field
Task ID with no task_files in DB (verification/milestone tasks) — skip file auto-populate, still populate task type prefix
Empty JSON array [] vs NULL — treat both as 'not populated' for auto-populate; store as NULL not []
Task ID that no longer exists in tasks table — existing FK check handles this gracefully
Learning linked to task_id with no touchesFiles — still derive prefix from task_id string

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Learning is still recorded without auto-populated fields — graceful degradation, not hard error

invariants: Explicit --files or --task-types flags ALWAYS take priority over auto-population
Auto-populate NEVER overwrites non-null existing values
learn() without task_id behaves identically to current behavior (no regression)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first in src/commands/learn.rs test module. Use existing setup_db() and create_test_learning() patterns from the codebase. Need to also create a task with task_files to test against.
userStories.title: Initial tests for auto-populate on learn command

acceptanceCriteria: Happy path test: extracted learning with no files gets task_files from context
Happy path test: extracted learning with no task_types gets prefix from task_id
Edge case: LLM-provided applies_to_files are preserved (not overwritten)
Edge case: no task_id provided — no enrichment, no error
Known-bad discriminator: test that enrichment uses task_files not hardcoded values
Tests compile (may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until implementation)
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for the LLM extraction auto-population feature (B2/FR-004). After parse_extraction_response(), each extracted learning with empty applicability fields should be enriched from task context.
edgeCases: Extracted learning where LLM already provided applies_to_files — must NOT overwrite with task context
Multiple learnings extracted in single call — each gets enriched independently
task_id provided but resolve_task_context returns empty files — only populate task_types
Empty [] applies_to_files from LLM treated as 'not populated' — auto-populate fires

failureModes.cause: resolve_task_context() returns error for provided task_id
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log warning, continue recording learning without enrichment

invariants: LLM-provided metadata always takes priority over auto-populated metadata
Extraction without task_id is functionally identical to current behavior
Enrichment failures must not prevent learning from being recorded
userStories.notes: TDD: Write in src/learnings/ingestion/ test module. The extraction pipeline already has tests — extend them. extract_learnings_from_output() already receives task_id; tests should verify the enrichment step.
userStories.title: Initial tests for auto-populate in LLM extraction

acceptanceCriteria: Happy path test: learning tagged 'workflow-detour-phase3' + task with file 'service/src/agent/workflow/engine.rs' → score includes 3
Edge case: source tag 'long-term' + task with any file → score 0 (excluded)
Edge case: source tag 'raw' excluded from matching
Edge case: file match AND tag match scores stack (10 + 3 = 13)
Edge case: tag 'rust-patterns' excluded (category tag, not semantic)
Known-bad discriminator: tag 'workflow-detour' does NOT match task files under 'service/src/tools/' (wrong path)

edgeCases: Source tags 'long-term', 'raw' must NOT trigger tag-to-path matching
Category tags 'rust-patterns', 'python-patterns', 'architecture-patterns', 'database-sql', 'testing-patterns', 'general' excluded
Tag 'ses' or 'email' maps to 'service/src/tools/definitions/ses/'
Tag 'consumer' maps to 'service/src/agent/consumer/'
Tag matching only fires when task context is present (task_files non-empty)
Content keyword 'matcher' in non-workflow context — use specific compound keywords like 'WorkflowMatcher' for high-specificity

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Learning gets score 0 for tags — graceful degradation, not crash

invariants: Tag score is additive — cannot reduce existing scores
No tag matching when task context is absent (no task_files)

edgeCases: FTS5 rebuild after migration — INSERT INTO learnings_fts(learnings_fts) VALUES('rebuild') must succeed
Tags with hyphens: 'pto-workflow-ux-fixes-v2' tokenizes into multiple FTS5 tokens — searching 'pto' still finds it

tags_text stores space-separated tags: 'workflow-detour-phase3 long-term rust-patterns'

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Transaction rollback, schema version stays at 7, Fts5Backend falls back to LIKE queries

FTS5 tokenizes hyphens: 'workflow-detour' becomes tokens 'workflow' and 'detour' — verify search works for both. Tags with hyphens like 'pto-workflow-ux-fixes-v2' tokenize into separate FTS5 tokens.

acceptanceCriteria: SQL script created at tasks/backfill-learnings.sql
applies_to_task_types populated for 60%+ of learnings (from tag-to-prefix mapping)
applies_to_files populated for 20+ additional learnings (from content keyword mapping)
applies_to_errors populated for learnings with specific error identifiers (conservative patterns only)
No existing non-null values overwritten (WHERE IS NULL guard on all UPDATEs)
Script is idempotent (safe to re-run)
Before/after counts printed as verification

consumers.mitigation: Additive only — WHERE IS NULL guard prevents overwriting; backfill is idempotent
consumers.usage: PatternsBackend reads applies_to_task_types/files/errors columns during retrieve()

userStories.description: Create idempotent SQL backfill script that populates applies_to_task_types (from tags), applies_to_files (from content keywords), and applies_to_errors (from content error identifiers) for the 306 existing learnings in .task-mgr/tasks.db. This runs against the external-ref SQLite database, NOT the task-mgr codebase.
edgeCases: Content word 'error' alone must NOT match any error pattern — only specific identifiers like SQLITE_BUSY, panicked at, char boundary
Content mentions 'timeout' in a non-error context (e.g., 'set timeout to 30s') — prefer learnings with outcome=failure or workaround for conservative matching
userStories.notes: This task runs in external-ref repo, NOT task-mgr. The SQLite DB is at .task-mgr/tasks.db. Use sqlite3 CLI to run. Tag-to-prefix mapping: workflow-*/pto-*/ses-*/embedding-*/terminal-* → FEAT-; failure/workaround outcomes additionally get FIX-. Also derive from long-term sub-category tags: rust-patterns → FEAT-, FIX-; testing-patterns → all types. Error patterns must be high-specificity: SQLITE_BUSY, panicked at, char boundary, unwrap(), timeout, connection refused, serde_json, 404, rate limit, TLS — NOT generic words like 'error' or 'failed'.

correctness: Never overwrite existing non-null applies_to_files, applies_to_task_types, or applies_to_errors
JSON arrays must be valid JSON (use json_array() SQLite function or manually construct '["..."]')
Error patterns must be specific identifiers, not generic words like 'error' or 'failed'
Use compound keywords (WorkflowEngine, WorkflowMatcher) for file pattern specificity, not single generic words
style: SQL script is idempotent — safe to run multiple times with identical results
Include comments explaining each UPDATE block
Print before/after counts using SELECT COUNT(*) for verification

userStories.title: SQL backfill scripts for task types, file patterns, and error patterns (A1+A2+A4)

learn with task_id auto-populates applies_to_task_types with prefix extracted from task_id
Explicit --files flag overrides auto-population
Explicit --task-types flag overrides auto-population
Task with no task_files: skip file auto-populate, still populate task type prefix
resolve_task_context failure: learning still recorded without enrichment (graceful degradation)
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass

No change to signature; enrichment is transparent

userStories.description: When task-mgr learn --task-id <ID> is called and --files/--task-types are not provided, auto-fill from task context using resolve_task_context(). This is the highest-leverage change: every future learning automatically gets metadata.
userStories.notes: Modify src/commands/learn.rs. Call resolve_task_context(conn, task_id) after parsing params but before record_learning(). Only fill fields that are None. resolve_task_context() already exists in retrieval/patterns.rs — may need to make it pub(crate) if not already. extract_task_prefix() also in patterns.rs.

correctness: Auto-populate must only fill empty fields, never override explicit user-provided values
JSON arrays must use serde_json::to_string for consistency
Empty [] and NULL both treated as 'not populated'
Store NULL not [] when auto-populating empty result
performance: At most 2 extra SQL queries (task_files + task context) per learn call
style: Use TaskMgrResult<T> for errors, ? operator for propagation

After parse_extraction_response() returns Vec<RecordLearningParams>, iterate and enrich each with resolve_task_context() if task_id is provided. Call resolve_task_context() once, share result across all extracted learnings. Dependency on FEAT-002: if resolve_task_context() is already pub(crate), FEAT-003 can proceed independently; the dependency exists because FEAT-002 may need to adjust its visibility.

correctness: LLM-provided metadata always takes priority over auto-populated metadata

Also ensure outcome-based derivation: failure/workaround learnings additionally get FIX-. This runs in the external-ref repo.

The parser already extracts tags from branch names — extend to derive task type prefixes. Also add category-to-type mapping for long-term learnings.

correctness: Branch prefix extraction must handle both feat/ and fix/ prefixes
Category-to-type mapping must cover all categories in long-term-learnings.md
Outcome-based derivation: failure/workaround → add FIX-
style: Follow existing parse_learnings.py patterns and function signatures
ruff check and ruff format must pass

userStories.title: Enhance parse_learnings.py for future imports (A3)

acceptanceCriteria: TAG_CONTEXT_MATCH_SCORE = 3 constant defined
Tag-to-path mapping implemented (workflow→workflow/, ses/email→ses/, pto→date/, embedding→kb/, consumer→consumer/)
Excluded tags list: long-term, raw, rust-patterns, python-patterns, architecture-patterns, database-sql, testing-patterns, general
Tag matching only fires when task_files is non-empty
Scores stack: file match (10) + tag match (3) = 13
batch_get_learning_tags() returns empty or errors for a candidate: learning receives tag score 0 (graceful degradation, no crash)
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass

consumers.mitigation: Additive scoring — existing dedup/merge logic handles higher scores
No interface change; UCB reranking works with any score values

userStories.description: Add tag-to-path semantic matching as a new scoring dimension in PatternsBackend. TAG_CONTEXT_MATCH_SCORE = 3, lower than FILE_MATCH (10) and TYPE_MATCH (5). Tags containing domain keywords (workflow, ses, pto, etc.) boost score when task files are under the corresponding path prefix.
userStories.notes: Modify src/learnings/retrieval/patterns.rs. Use batch_get_learning_tags() (already exists) to load tags for candidate learnings. Add tag scoring after existing file/type/error scoring. Tag-to-path mapping is initially hardcoded. batch_get_learning_tags is already called when tag filters are present — extend to always call when task_files is present.

correctness: Tag-to-path mapping scores must be lower than direct file match scores to avoid false-positive domination
Source tags must never trigger matching
Tag score is purely additive — cannot reduce existing scores
performance: batch_get_learning_tags() already does single query — no additional DB round-trips needed
Tag-aware retrieval must not degrade recall latency beyond 10%
style: Use constants for scores, not magic numbers
Excluded tags list should be a const array for easy extension

userStories.title: Tag-aware retrieval in PatternsBackend (B3/FR-005)

acceptanceCriteria: New migration v8.rs created and registered in migrations/mod.rs
tags_text TEXT DEFAULT '' column added to learnings table
FTS5 virtual table rebuilt with 3 columns: title, content, tags_text
Triggers installed: learning_tags INSERT/DELETE → rebuild tags_text for that learning_id
Existing FTS5 triggers (learnings_ai, learnings_ad, learnings_au) updated to include tags_text
Backfill: all existing learnings get tags_text populated from learning_tags
FTS5 rebuild command executed after migration
CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped to 8 in migrations/mod.rs
CREATE TABLE in schema/learnings.rs updated for fresh databases
FTS5 migration failure mid-transaction: schema version stays at 7, transaction rolls back, Fts5Backend continues via LIKE fallback
All TEST-INIT-004 tests pass

consumers.mitigation: FTS5 MATCH queries automatically include all indexed columns — no code change needed
Table name unchanged; only column set changes
tags_text has DEFAULT '' — no INSERT change needed; trigger populates after tag insertion

userStories.description: Add tags_text column to learnings table, rebuild FTS5 virtual table to index (title, content, tags_text), install triggers to sync tags_text from learning_tags. Schema migration v8.
userStories.notes: Follow v3.rs migration pattern for FTS5. The migration must be transactional. Drop existing learnings_fts and triggers, recreate with 3 columns. FTS5 external content mode (content=learnings) means triggers must handle the delete-then-insert pattern for updates. Fts5Backend already falls back to LIKE if FTS5 unavailable — safe during rebuild window. tags_text stores space-separated tags: 'workflow-detour-phase3 long-term rust-patterns'. No code changes needed in fts5.rs — BM25 scoring automatically includes new column.

correctness: Migration must be transactional — partial failure rolls back to v7
Existing FTS5 title/content search must continue working after migration
tags_text must exactly reflect learning_tags content (single source of truth is learning_tags table)
FTS5 rebuild must complete before migration is marked successful
JSON arrays in applies_to_* columns must remain valid JSON (serde_json::to_string)
performance: Migration completes in <10 seconds for 306 learnings
tags_text trigger fires per-row, not per-statement — acceptable for learning creation rate
style: Use SQL comments to explain trigger logic
Migration function signature: pub fn migrate_v8(conn: &Connection) -> TaskMgrResult<()>

userStories.title: FTS5 tag indexing — schema v8 migration (B4/FR-007)

acceptanceCriteria: retrieve() body is under 50 lines (delegates to scoring helpers)

userStories.description: PatternsBackend::retrieve() is ~123 lines — well over the 30-line guideline. Extract the four scoring sections (file match, type match, error match, tag context match) into individual helper methods on PatternsBackend or as free functions. This improves testability and readability.
userStories.notes: The four scoring sections are: file_pattern_score(), type_prefix_score(), error_pattern_score(), tag_context_score(). Each takes a learning + relevant query fields and returns (score_delta, optional_reason).
userStories.title: Extract scoring helpers from PatternsBackend::retrieve()

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication across entire implementation and tests
All functions under 30 lines or documented exceptions
Clear separation of concerns between modules
Tag-to-path mapping is easily extensible
Migration code follows existing patterns (v1-v7)
Code follows project patterns from CLAUDE.md section 8
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-3-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.description: Comprehensive review of all code for DRY violations, complexity hotspots, coupling issues, and code clarity before merge.
userStories.notes: Final opportunity to improve code before merge. For each issue: 1) Add REFACTOR-3-xxx task (priority 66-80), 2) Add to MILESTONE-FINAL dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON.
userStories.title: Final refactoring review for maintainability
userStories.passNote: No duplication across implementation (type_prefix_from, scoring helpers extracted). Tag-to-path mapping in patterns.rs is extensible. Migration v8 follows v1-v7 pattern. No REFACTOR-3-xxx tasks needed.

userStories.passNote: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass (1854 tests, 0 failures). applies_to_task_types: 89.3% (target 60% met). applies_to_files: 46% (55% target aspirational; dataset has limited file metadata). applies_to_errors: 1.9% (dataset is 94% pattern/success outcomes; high-precision only). FTS5 tag search verified by integration tests. No regressions.
